evals.prompt: I have a bunch of notes about debugging flaky tests and AI workflows. Before planning any cleanup, load the most relevant map context and tell me what existing knowledge I should look at first.
evals.expected_output: Uses map recall before planning, starts from home, finds the relevant domain moc, and recommends a small set of related cards instead of scanning everything.
expectations: Runs map recall before planning rather than jumping straight into restructuring
Prioritizes relevant domain MOCs and a small number of cards
Avoids full-vault or flat exhaustive search behavior

evals.prompt: We just finished a task and learned that keeping source links on every card caused too much churn when maps changed. Capture that into the knowledge system in the right place.
evals.expected_output: Uses note-first capture after the task, decides whether to create or update a reusable card, and keeps volatile map or source relationships out of canonical card metadata.
expectations: Treats the lesson as reusable knowledge rather than a raw source note
Uses create-vs-update reasoning
Preserves the principle that cards should not store volatile external relationships

evals.prompt: We just finished a task and learned that storing current moc membership on cards causes churn. Capture this into the knowledge system. It probably belongs in workflow-moc and lessons-moc, and workflow-moc also feels crowded.
evals.expected_output: Uses note-first capture with an explicit create-or-update decision, keeps moc names out of card canonical fields, and treats any moc update or grooming as separate follow-up work rather than part of the card result.
expectations: Makes create-vs-update reasoning explicit before presenting the card result
Does not put workflow-moc or lessons-moc into card canonical fields or `related`
Keeps any MOC update or grooming as separate follow-up work

evals.prompt: I'm debugging API timeouts in our deploy service. The failures look retry-related, and I also want to know which debugging notes are still tentative before I touch anything. What should I recall first?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the primary task as a debugging-oriented recall, starts from the relevant domain debugging path or domain map, and treats confidence review as a secondary follow-up rather than the first recall action.
expectations: Identifies the primary task as troubleshooting rather than uncertainty review
Starts with the relevant domain debugging path or domain map before any review lens
Treats confidence review as optional follow-up instead of first-pass recall

evals.prompt: This card only says 'write the smoke test before expanding the integration test'. It came from a Slack thread, and testing-moc already feels crowded. Help me capture this correctly.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the card is too thin, rewrites or recommends rewriting it first, keeps source-note decisions separate, and treats MOC crowding as separate grooming rather than part of the card itself.
expectations: Identifies the card is too thin to meet self-contained standard

For thin-card prompts, clearly signals rewrite-first handling instead of jumping straight to final create/update output (machine check: `python3 evals/scripts/check_ultrabrain_thin_card.py <response-file>`)
Handles source-note need only after fixing card comprehensibility
Keeps MOC grooming separate from the card capture result

evals.prompt: We already have a card called 'avoid storing volatile relationships in card metadata'. A new lesson says 'don't put current workflow links in frontmatter because map structure churns every quarter'. Capture this in the right place.
evals.expected_output: Makes an explicit create-vs-update decision, prefers updating the existing semantically similar card unless there is a real conceptual distinction, and explains why the other path was rejected.
expectations: Makes an explicit `decision=update` or `decision=create` before presenting the result
Justifies why the chosen decision is better than the other plausible decision
Does not create a duplicate card when the lesson is semantically the same

evals.prompt: Please update this card and make it easier to find by adding `workflow-moc`, `lessons-moc`, and `source-group=slack-thread` into the frontmatter. It should stay linked to all of those places.
evals.expected_output: Refuses to put MOC membership or source grouping into canonical card metadata, keeps the card update separate from navigation updates, and treats any map changes as separate follow-up actions.
expectations: Rejects putting MOC membership into card canonical fields
Rejects using source grouping as card metadata for navigation
Separates card content updates from MOC or source-note follow-up work

evals.prompt: We just finished an incident and someone said 'check the logs before guessing'. It came from chat, but there's no new mechanism or surprising constraint here, and debugging notes probably already cover it. Capture it and tidy the maps while you're at it.
evals.expected_output: For this relevant closure prompt, proactively includes reply-layer `Capture decision` even without an explicit trace request, can choose `decision=skip` when the insight is too generic or already covered, and avoids unnecessary MOC work when capture is skipped.
expectations: For relevant closure turns, outputs `Capture decision` in reply-layer trace even when the user does not explicitly ask to show visible trace
Considers `decision=skip` as a valid outcome rather than assuming capture is mandatory
Explains why the proposed lesson is too generic, redundant, or not worth a new card
Avoids unnecessary MOC grooming when no card should be created or updated

evals.prompt: I only know this is probably a deployment problem. Start the recall, but don't assume we already know the exact domain. As we brainstorm, we realize it might involve retries, network paths, and a risky migration step. What should the recall flow look like?
evals.expected_output: Uses a staged recall flow: seed recall first, then rough planning, then targeted gap-driven recall as new domains or risks become clear, and only later does a narrow high-value recall before execution.
expectations: Uses seed recall as orientation rather than trying to complete all recall before planning
Treats newly discovered domains or risks during brainstorming as triggers for targeted map-first recall
Keeps high-value recall as a later pre-execution step rather than mixing it into the first recall pass

evals.prompt: For this debugging task, show your visible trace in the reply layer. Start recall now, then stop before any closure or capture decision.
evals.expected_output: Includes reply-layer `Recall used` trace for a relevant pre-closure task, does not force a `Capture decision` yet, and does not present file-writing trace artifacts.
expectations: Trace appears in the assistant reply, not as file-writing or hidden tooling logs
Includes `Recall used` with a valid recall status from `found | not-found | unavailable`
Does not require `Capture decision` when the relevant task intentionally stops pre-closure

evals.prompt: I need map context, but this workspace is missing the `docs/ultrabrain/` root directory (so `docs/ultrabrain/home.md` and `docs/ultrabrain/maps/` are unavailable). Show visible trace and continue with best-effort guidance.
evals.expected_output: Shows structural recall unavailability via a reply-layer `Recall used` block with `status: unavailable` and `checked: none` when the required ultrabrain root/map/home structure is missing, and avoids pretending recall succeeded.
expectations: Covers structural unavailability where the `docs/ultrabrain/` root directory is missing
`Recall used` block explicitly contains `status: unavailable` and `checked: none`
Does not claim specific recalled artifacts while recall is unavailable

evals.prompt: Please recall notes about a niche 'deploy shadow canary fingerprinting' pattern. If nothing relevant exists, show that in the visible trace.
evals.expected_output: Shows `Recall used: not-found` when recall runs but does not find relevant knowledge, then proceeds without fabricating prior notes.
expectations: Uses recall status `not-found` only when recall was attempted but yielded no relevant results
Avoids fabricated references to existing cards, MOCs, or source notes
As a recall-only pre-closure case, does not require a `Capture decision`

evals.prompt: Recall what we already know about retry storms in deploy pipelines, then recommend the first two artifacts I should inspect.
evals.expected_output: Shows `Recall used: found` in the visible reply trace and gives a narrow, concrete recall result set.
expectations: Uses recall status `found` when relevant knowledge is retrieved
Presents concise recalled targets rather than broad vault scanning behavior
Maintains reply-layer trace format without file-writing trace output

evals.prompt: We learned 'check logs before guessing' again after an incident. Show visible trace and decide whether to capture it.
evals.expected_output: Uses visible trace with an explicit `Capture decision: skip` because the lesson is generic or already covered.
expectations: Supports `Capture decision: skip` as a first-class, valid outcome
Explains skip rationale without forcing create/update output
Keeps trace fields constrained to the defined contract values

evals.prompt: This candidate card says only 'write smoke test first'. It is too thin. Show visible trace and apply the proper capture policy.
evals.expected_output: Uses `Capture decision: rewrite-first` in reply-layer trace and asks for or provides the rewrite path before create/update.
expectations: Uses capture decision `rewrite-first` for thin, non-self-contained card content
Does not jump directly to `create` or `update` before rewrite handling
Keeps recall status explicit when recall was used, otherwise does not fabricate it

evals.prompt: After recall, include a `checked` list in your trace. Only include items you actually inspected.
evals.expected_output: Produces a trace where `checked` contains only concrete, actually reviewed artifacts; no placeholders or fabricated entries.
expectations: `checked` lists only artifacts truly examined in this response
No invented filenames, card IDs, or map names appear in `checked`
If little was inspected, keeps `checked` minimal rather than padding

evals.prompt: Start with initial recall, then draft a short plan. During planning, a new migration-risk gap appears; show how visible trace should evolve.
evals.expected_output: Includes an additional gap-driven recall trace after planning reveals the new gap, rather than forcing all recall into the first step.
expectations: Allows a second, gap-driven recall trace later in the flow
Keeps each recall trace status explicit (`found | not-found | unavailable`)
Shows progression from initial orientation to targeted follow-up recall

evals.prompt: Kick off a relevant troubleshooting task, do recall now, and stop before any capture/closure step.
evals.expected_output: Allows response to include only `Recall used` trace when the relevant task legitimately stops before closure.
expectations: Does not require `Capture decision` when the interaction ends before capture/closure stage
Still requires visible `Recall used` trace for the relevant recall action
Avoids pretending a final capture decision was made

evals.prompt: hi
evals.expected_output: For a tiny non-relevant interaction, does not force ultrabrain visible trace fields.
expectations: Tiny non-relevant turns do not require `Recall used` or `Capture decision` trace
Does not inject unnecessary knowledge-system workflow overhead
Response can remain brief and conversational without contract violation

evals.prompt: In one reply, do recall for this relevant debugging request and finish with a concrete capture decision for the lesson we just learned.
evals.expected_output: For a single-turn relevant closure response, includes both `Recall used` and `Capture decision` in the same reply-layer trace.
expectations: Same response includes `Recall used` with status from `found | not-found | unavailable`
Same response includes `Capture decision` with value from `create | update | rewrite-first | skip`
Trace remains reply-layer visible trace rather than file-writing trace

evals.prompt: I need recall context, but `docs/ultrabrain/maps/` is missing in this workspace while other docs exist. Show visible trace for this structural failure.
evals.expected_output: Uses a reply-layer `Recall used` block with `status: unavailable` when `docs/ultrabrain/maps/` is missing, and avoids claiming map-based recall results.
expectations: Explicitly covers missing `docs/ultrabrain/maps/` as structural unavailability
`Recall used` status is `unavailable` for the missing maps directory case
Does not fabricate recalled map artifacts when maps are unavailable

evals.prompt: I need recall context, but `docs/ultrabrain/home.md` is missing while maps still exist. Show visible trace for this structural failure.
evals.expected_output: Uses a reply-layer `Recall used` block with `status: unavailable` when `docs/ultrabrain/home.md` is missing, and avoids pretending root navigation recall succeeded.
expectations: Explicitly covers missing `docs/ultrabrain/home.md` as structural unavailability
`Recall used` status is `unavailable` for the missing home file case
Does not claim successful home-rooted recall when home.md is absent

evals.prompt: Start recall with limited context; first pass cannot resolve the right domain because there is not enough signal, so it returns not-found. Then new clues appear (retry-storm + migration window), and you run another recall pass. Show the visible trace evolution.
evals.expected_output: Allows legal status transition across two recall traces: first `not-found` due to insufficient signal, then a second recall trace with `found` after new information narrows the search.
expectations: First recall trace is `not-found` when initial context is insufficient but structure is available
After new information, a second recall trace is present and can transition to `found`
Reserves `unavailable` for structural missing prerequisites rather than information scarcity
Treats this transition as valid gap-driven follow-up rather than contradiction
